name: Eddie Johnson
description: American jazz and blues tenor saxophonist (1920–2010)
abstract: Edwin Lawrence Johnson (December 11, 1920 – April 7, 2010) was an American jazz and blues tenor saxophonist.

about_quotes.speaker: Quinn Wilson
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Eddie Johnson with the -- Merle Young's band at the Rum Boogie, during the war.

about_quotes.speaker: Josiah 'Cie' Frazier
about_quotes.evidence: Now that band that consisted of during them times... Eddie Johnson on tenor... and I was on the drum.

about_quotes.speaker: John "Picky" Brunious
about_quotes.evidence: My brother-in-law, Eddie Johnson, was playing saxophone... I was playing trumpet.

about_quotes.speaker: Ernest McLean
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions 'Eddie Johnson' and 'Big Eddie Johnson' / 'Little Eddie Johnson' while showing photos, but the interviewee (Ernest McLean) does not respond with any personal connection, memory, or statement indicating he knew or met him.

about_quotes.speaker: Edgar "Sambo" Joseph
about_quotes.evidence: I had Eddie Johnson with me... I had Eddie Johnson. I had Manuel Serra's on Ghetto. They didn't use these bands. They used Ghetto on Converse Show. Bigfoot, Billy, myself. We played all that, man.

about_quotes.speaker: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions 'Eddie Johnson' and 'Big Ed' as 'my buddy', but the interviewee does not respond with any personal connection or memory regarding Eddie Johnson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Billie Pierce and De De Pierce Interview, 1959-10-07
